title: FUGUEの実装を確認し、Kernelとの差分を埋めて。ただしFUGUE/kernel両方の既存のシステムを損壊しないように慎重に実装をマルチエージェントで進めて。
summary_text: **Findings (by severity)** 1. **High: `no-op` contract violation risk via compact artifact creation from local FUGUE path** `run-local-orchestration.sh` now calls `kernel-compact-artifact.sh update` whenever `KERNEL_RUN_ID` is set, without checking that a compact artifact already exists. - Call site: [run-local-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh#L1119) - `update` path writes a new compact file even when absent: [kernel-compact-artifact.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh#L404), [kernel-compact-artifact.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh#L604) - This conflicts with the documented rule: compact missing must be `no-op` and must not create new artifact: [requirements-phase-artifact-contract-v1.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/requirements-phase-artifact-contract-v1.md#L206), [requirements-phase-artifact-contract-v1.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/requirements-phase-artifact-contract-v1.md#L212) 2. **High: boundary drift from provider-specific memory hints into shared compact context** `claude_resume_hint` (provider-specific session continuation detail) is captured into the FUGUE context reference, then injected into Kernel compact/prompt surface. - Capture: [fugue-context-reference.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/fugue-context-reference.sh#L78) - Propagation to compact summary: [run-local-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh#L1124) - Rendered in Kernel prompt: [kernel-codex-thread.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-codex-thread.sh#L69) - This conflicts with “provider-agnostic only” / “path-only handoff surface” intent: [requirements-phase-artifact-contract-v1.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/requirements-phase-artifact-contract-v1.md#L66), [requirements-phase-artifact-contract-v1.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/requirements-phase-artifact-contract-v1.md#L89) 3. **Medium: single-writer traceability contract is bypassed by a new local writer path** The documentation freezes workflow-side propagation as single-writer; this change adds a second writer (`run-local-orchestration.sh` -> compact update). - New writer path: [run-local-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh#L1127) - Single-writer contract: [requirements-phase-artifact-contract-v1.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/requirements-phase-artifact-contract-v1.md#L283) **What I validated** - Targeted regressions passed: `test-local-orchestration-consensus-bridge.sh`, `test-kernel-handoff-summary.sh`, `test-kernel-compact-artifact.sh`, `test-kernel-codex-thread.sh`, `test-fugue-context-reference.sh`. - I did not run the full repository test suite, so broader integration impact remains unverified.
